active.noneDesc: Your RabbitMQ cluster is running smoothly with no alerts.
active.upgradeSubtitle: Upgrade to view detailed alert information and configure thresholds.
active.upgradeHint: Developer and Enterprise unlock alert details, rules, and notification channels.

failedToLoad: Failed to load alerts data. Please try again.
failedToLoadResolved: Failed to load resolved alerts. Please try again.
noAlertsResolvedYet: No alerts have been resolved yet.
rules.title: Alert Rules
rules.description: Manage your custom alert rules for monitoring RabbitMQ metrics
rules.selectServer: Please select a server to view alert rules
rules.noRulesDescription: Create custom alert rules to monitor specific metrics
rules.editDescription: Update your custom alert rule configuration
rules.createDescription: Create a custom alert rule to monitor specific RabbitMQ metrics
rules.form.name: Name *
rules.delete.title: Delete Alert Rule
rules.delete.confirmation: Are you sure you want to delete the alert rule "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
rules.searchPlaceholder: Search rules by name or type...

modal.title: Alert Notification Settings
modal.description: Configure email and webhook notifications for new alerts. Select which alert severities you want to receive notifications for.
modal.serversDescription: Select which servers you want to receive notifications for. If none are selected, notifications will be sent for all servers.
modal.searchPlaceholder: Search servers by name, host, or port...
modal.serverSelectionCount: {{selected}} of {{total}} server selected. Leave empty to receive notifications for all servers.
modal.serverSelectionCount_other: {{selected}} of {{total}} servers selected. Leave empty to receive notifications for all servers.
modal.alertSeveritiesDescription: Select which alert severities you want to receive notifications for (applies to email, webhook, and Slack notifications)
modal.selectAtLeastOneSeverity: Please select at least one alert severity
modal.browserNotificationsDescription: Receive browser notifications for new alerts. You'll be prompted to allow notifications when you enable this feature.
modal.browserNotificationSeveritiesDescription: Select which alert severities you want to receive browser notifications for
modal.selectAtLeastOneBrowserSeverity: Please select at least one alert severity for browser notifications
modal.browserNotificationsDisabled: Browser notifications are disabled. Enable this feature to receive desktop notifications for new alerts.
modal.browserNotificationsBlocked: Browser notifications are blocked. To enable them, click the lock icon in your browser's address bar and allow notifications for this site.
modal.browserNotificationsSystemHint: Not seeing notifications? Make sure notifications for your browser are also enabled in your operating system settings (e.g. macOS: System Settings → Notifications → Chrome).
modal.emailNotificationsDescription: Receive email alerts for new alerts based on your severity preferences
modal.emailHelp: This email will receive notifications for new alerts
modal.emailNotificationsDisabled: Email notifications are disabled. You won't receive alerts via email, but you can still view them in the dashboard.
modal.webhookDescription: Configure webhook endpoint to receive alert notifications via POST requests.
modal.webhookSecretHelp: Optional secret key for webhook signature verification
modal.slackDescription: Configure Slack webhook to receive alert notifications in your Slack channels.
modal.slackWebhookHelp: You can get Slack Incoming Webhook URL in
modal.webhookPayloadExampleDescription: This is an example of the JSON payload that will be sent to your webhook endpoint when alerts are triggered.
modal.webhookSignatureNote: Note: If you configure a secret, the payload will include an X-Qarote-Signature header with an HMAC-SHA256 signature. You can use this to verify the authenticity of the webhook request.
modal.webhookSignatureNoteText: If you configure a secret, the payload will include an
modal.webhookSignatureNoteSuffix: header with an HMAC-SHA256 signature. You can use this to verify the authenticity of the webhook request.
modal.toastEmailRequired: Please provide an email address for notifications
modal.toastEmailInvalid: Please provide a valid email address
modal.toastSelectSeverity: Please select at least one alert severity
modal.toastSelectBrowserSeverity: Please select at least one alert severity for browser notifications
modal.toastWebhookUrlInvalid: Please provide a valid webhook URL
modal.toastWebhookSlackError: Slack webhook URLs should be added in the Slack Notifications section, not here.
modal.toastSlackUrlRequired: Please provide a Slack webhook URL
modal.toastSlackUrlInvalid: Invalid Slack webhook URL. Must be in the format: https://hooks.slack.com/services/
